channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from trusted sources

item.title: Zelensky warns Russia may launch northern Ukraine offensive
item.description: Ukraine president says Russia may be preparing a new offensive toward Kyiv from the Chernihiv direction, potentially with Belarusian involvement, as concerns grow about expanding front lines.

item.title: Syria invited to G7 summit in France as guest nation
item.description: Syria will attend the G7 summit in France next month for the first time, with President Ahmed al-Sharaa representing the country in a major diplomatic breakthrough following years of isolation.

item.title: All Gaza flotilla activists released from Israeli detention
item.description: Rights group Adalah confirms all participants from the Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla have been released from Israeli detention ahead of deportation, with Turkey arranging special flights for citizens.

item.title: Merz proposes radical EU membership shakeup for Ukraine, Moldova
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz says the EU enlargement process takes too long and creates frustration, proposing a new framework to accelerate membership for Ukraine, Moldova, and Western Balkans.

item.title: Wes Streeting pledges wealth tax in Labour leadership challenge
item.description: UK Health Secretary Wes Streeting pledges to introduce a wealth tax if elected Labour leader, directly challenging PM Keir Starmer as pressure mounts on the prime minister.

item.title: Austrian Ex-Intelligence Officer Convicted of Spying for Russia
item.description: Egisto Ott, a former Austrian intelligence official, was sentenced to four years for passing secret information to Russian intelligence and fugitive Wirecard executive Jan Marsalek. The case has revived concerns about Austria as a hub for Russian espionage.

item.title: US Surveillance Flights Track Near Cuba Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Flight-tracking data shows US Navy P-8A Poseidon jets and MQ-4C Triton drones operating within 50 miles of Cuba as Washington maintains pressure on the islands communist government following an effective oil blockade.

item.title: Israeli Minister Condemned for Taunting Detained Gaza Flotilla Activists
item.description: Far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir posted a video taunting handcuffed pro-Palestinian activists from a Gaza-bound aid flotilla intercepted by Israeli forces, drawing criticism from France, Italy, and even Prime Minister Netanyahu.

item.title: Sierra Leone Receives US Deportees Under Trump Immigration Crackdown
item.description: Nine West African migrants were flown to Sierra Leone as part of Donald Trumps mass deportation policy. The country agreed to accept up to 300 deportees annually, joining other African nations receiving expelled migrants.

item.title: US Threatens to Revoke Palestinian UN Delegation Visas
item.description: A State Department cable instructed US diplomats to pressure Palestinian officials to withdraw Ambassador Riyad Mansour candidacy for UN General Assembly vice president or face visa revocation. The Trump administration previously barred Palestinian officials from attending the UN General Assembly last year.

item.title: China-Linked Vessel Suspected in Taiwan Submarine Cable Cut
item.description: A submarine cable linking Matsu Island to Taiwan was severed in April, the third incident in four years. Analysis points to a Chinese dredger vessel with maritime militia ties operating under the pretense of a salvage operation. Taiwan faces infrastructure vulnerability as Chinese gray-zone tactics intensify.

item.title: Lithuanian Lawmakers Shelter Underground as Drone Violates Airspace
item.description: A drone violated Lithuanian airspace, forcing lawmakers to shelter underground and temporarily suspending flights at Vilnius airport. The incident is the latest in a series of security concerns in the Baltic region.

item.title: Global Oil Market Faces Supply Crunch Within Months Without Peace Deal
item.description: The oil industry has shown resilience during the Iran conflict, but analysts warn the global market may be only months away from a breaking point if peace talks fail to produce a breakthrough.

item.title: UAE Pipeline Bypassing Hormuz 50% Complete, ADNOC CEO Says
item.description: The UAEs new crude pipeline that bypasses the Strait of Hormuz is about 50% complete, with the head of ADNOC warning that global oil flows may take at least four months to recover after the Iran war ends.

item.title: Samsung Averts Strike with Last-Minute Wage Deal, Bonuses Up to $416,000
item.description: Samsung Electronics reached an 11th-hour wage deal with its South Korean union, averting a strike. Shares surged, though concerns remain about the large bonuses included in the agreement.

item.title: Bank of Japan Policymaker Calls for Rate Hike, Cites War-Driven Inflation Risk
item.description: BOJ board member Junko Koeda said the central bank should raise rates at an "appropriate pace," warning that price pressures from the Middle East war could push inflation above the 2% target.

item.title: Russia Delivers Nuclear Munitions to Belarus for Drills
item.description: Russia has delivered nuclear munitions to field storage facilities in Belarus as part of major nuclear exercises, escalating tensions with the West over Ukraine.

item.title: Germany Proposes Associate EU Membership for Ukraine
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has proposed giving Ukraine a direct role in EU structures as an interim step toward full membership, potentially helping facilitate a deal to end the war with Russia.

item.title: Iran Reviewing US Peace Proposal as Trump Warns of Further Strikes
item.description: Tehran is reviewing Washington's latest response to a ceasefire proposal, with Trump saying negotiations are in final stages but threatening renewed attacks if no deal is reached.

item.title: Russian Oil and Gas Revenue Surges 39% Due to Iran War Price Rally
item.description: Russia's state oil and gas revenues are projected to rise 39% year-on-year in May to 700 billion roubles, driven by global oil price increases from the Iran conflict.

item.title: Central Russian Oil Refineries Halt Output After Ukrainian Drone Strikes
item.description: Major oil refineries in central Russia have been forced to stop or reduce fuel production following Ukrainian drone attacks, according to official data and industry sources.

item.title: Japan Dismisses Russia-China Claims of Remilitarization
item.description: Tokyo rejected a joint statement by Presidents Xi and Putin accusing Japan of accelerating remilitarization. The dispute comes as Japan strengthens defense ties with South Korea amid China's growing influence.

item.title: Multiple Nations Summon Israeli Envoys Over Flotilla Activist Treatment
item.description: France, Netherlands, Canada, Ireland, Spain, Italy, Germany, Greece, Britain, Turkey and New Zealand summoned Israeli ambassadors over National Security Minister Ben-Gvir's video showing detained Gaza flotilla activists kneeling and tied. Netanyahu criticized the minister's actions.

item.title: Rare Ebola strain spreads rapidly in eastern Congo as health workers struggle
item.description: The Bundibugyo virus, a rare type of Ebola, has spread undetected for weeks in eastern Congo, with 139 suspected deaths and 600 cases. WHO says cases may exceed 1,000, with a vaccine months away amid conflict zones and underfunded health systems.

item.title: US military surveillance flights track near Cuba as tensions mount
item.description: BBC Verify analysis shows at least five US Navy P-8A Poseidon jets and three MQ-4C Triton drones have operated near Cuba since May 11, with flights as close as 50 miles from the island. Experts say the visible flight transponders are likely a deliberate signal to enforce the oil blockade and deter Venezuela.
